short_summary:
Thomas Jonathan  Ossoff (  OSS-off; born February 16, 1987) is an American politician who has served as the senior United States senator from Georgia since 2021. He is the youngest incumbent U.S. senator, and a member of the Democratic Party.
Previously, Ossoff worked as a national security staffer and legislative assistant for U.S. representative Hank Johnson. Afterward, he was managing director of an investigative production company. He first ran for Congress in a 2017 special election, narrowly losing a seat that had long been considered a Republican stronghold. 

Ossoff was elected to the U.S. Senate in 2021, defeating incumbent Republican David Perdue in a runoff. Ossoff's election alongside Raphael Warnock on the same day was critical in securing a 50–50 Senate majority for Democrats, with Vice President Kamala Harris serving as the tie-breaking vote. Ossoff is the youngest person elected to the Senate since 1980, the first senator born in the 1980s, and the first millennial United States senator. Alongside Warnock, he is one of only two Democrats to represent Georgia in the United States Senate since Zell Miller left office in 2005.
